_meta.title: Singapore residential market — curated baseline
_meta.not_advice: Educational baseline from primary public sources (URA, HDB, MAS, SingStat) plus portal aggregations where no official figure exists. Portal-sourced / computed / flash values are flagged. pipeline.py overlays data/live.json (data.gov.sg + URA GLS) where present; without it this baseline stands. Not financial advice.

hdb_rpi.label: HDB Resale Price Index
hdb_rpi.source: HDB releases; Q2-26 flash 1 Jul 2026 (data.gov.sg to Q1-26)

OCR.label: Outside Central Region (suburban / mass-market)
OCR.median_psf_flag: portal aggregation, not a URA median
OCR.note: Best gross yield and the only segment where the $1.2-1.5m 2-bed brief fits comfortably (resale). Suburban demand anchored by upgrader / new-household formation.

RCR.label: Rest of Central Region (city fringe)
RCR.note: Above-budget for 2-bed resale (avg ~$1.64m) except older / fringe stock — D14 (Geylang / Eunos) is the main value pocket.

CCR.label: Core Central Region (prime)
CCR.note: Lowest yield; within the $1.2-1.5m budget only 1-bedders fit. Leading Q2 2026 on price after years of underperformance.

Landed.label: Landed housing
Landed.note: Scarce, citizen-only, lowest yield and least liquid, but structurally supply-constrained. Outside the budget and the liquidity brief.

HDB.label: HDB resale
HDB.note: Highest gross yield and most liquid by volume, but MOP/eligibility/ethnic-quota rules, MSR cap, and no ABSD-free 2nd-property leverage. Mild two-quarter price decline into 2026.

quantum_2br.note: Average transacted quantum for 2-bedroom non-landed, full-year 2025 (Stacked Homes, 13 Jan 2026 — best available baseline; add ~3-4% for 2026 drift). Sizes in sqft.

gls.note: Government Land Sales residential tenders. Land bid $psf per plot ratio (ppr) signals developer conviction and sets a forward floor under nearby new-launch and resale prices. Number of bids gauges competition. Overlaid live from the URA Past-Sale-Sites workbook where available.
gls.source: URA Past-Sale-Sites workbook; curated baseline from Stacked Homes / 99.co (2026)
gls.trend_note: Land rates rising year on year on 3-6 disciplined bids per tender — participation steady, margins tight. Land cost is the sticky floor while resale prices plateau.

land_to_launch.note: Rule-of-thumb forward launch price from a winning land bid, derived from 2025-26 public land-vs-launch pairs. Launch psf ~= land psf ppr x factor (covers construction, financing, margin). Flagged heuristic, not a valuation.

ec_policy_2026.note: EC policy fine-tuning; applies only to EC GLS sites with tender closing on/after 8 May 2026. Existing pipeline and already-launched 2026 ECs keep the old 5-year MOP + deferred-payment scheme.

brief.label: Worked example — 42-year-old, S$1.2-1.5m 2-bed condo

fit_summary.best_fit: Resale OCR 2-bedder ~$1.25-1.4m, 700-800 sqft (D18 Tampines, D19 Hougang/Sengkang/Punggol, D22 Jurong, D23 Bukit Batok/Panjang, D25 Woodlands, D27 Sembawang/Yishun).
fit_summary.stretch: New-launch OCR 2-bedder at $2,100-2,200 psf, ~650-700 sqft, ~$1.4-1.6m (top of budget).
fit_summary.three_bed: Resale OCR 3-bedder possible in older stock, ~$1.35-1.5m for 1,000-1,100 sqft; new-launch OCR 3-bed ~$1.9m+ is out of budget.
fit_summary.rcr_value_pocket: D14 (Geylang / Eunos) fringe resale is the main RCR pocket that occasionally fits.
fit_summary.ccr: Only 1-bedders fit the budget in prime districts.

brief.affordability_note: At the 4% TDSR floor, 75% LTV, 30-year tenure, an OCR 2BR at ~$1.4m needs roughly $9,150/month income and ~$464k upfront (incl. ~$40.5k BSD). At age 42, full 75% LTV caps tenure at 23 years, which raises the instalment vs a 30-year loan — see the Affordability tab.
